hero.titleLead: Fifteen minutes with an operator who has
hero.lede: Direct access to Rengan Rajaratnam, three decades in global finance across Morgan Stanley, SAC Capital, Mercer, and Robertson Stephens, on your actual situation. A growth question, a thesis you are testing, a decision you are weighing. An introductory call to start, and where it goes from there is up to you.
hero.credLine: Morgan Stanley · SAC Capital · Mercer · Robertson Stephens

cards.title: An operator's read
cards.body: Not generic advice. A direct, experienced read on your actual situation, from someone who has built companies and sat across the table from them.

cards.title: A clear point of view
cards.body: Bring two or three questions. Leave with a sharp, honest answer on each, and a clear sense of your next move.

why.body: Three decades of global finance experience, including Morgan Stanley, SAC Capital, Mercer, and Robertson Stephens. A Stanford MBA. Years building and backing companies, and a network across founders, funds, and operators that is hard to reach from the outside. For fifteen minutes, you have his full attention, directly.

questions.title: What people bring to the call.

items.title: Where to bet
items.body: “We have two product lines and the team for one. The easy one pays the bills, the big one is the bet. Which do we starve?”

items.title: Timing a raise
items.body: “A name-brand fund is interested but moving slowly, and we have term sheets from smaller names now. Do we wait, or build the round around who is ready?”

items.title: Before you wire
items.body: “We are about to lead a round. The founder is excellent and the numbers are thin. What would you stress-test before we commit?”

items.title: Before diligence
items.body: “Diligence starts in six weeks and our reporting will not survive it. What do we fix first, and what can wait?”

longer.body: Some founders and funds come back. The work grows into strategy, growth, and capital, measured in months and quarters. It is selective, and built around the relationship itself. It begins the same way for everyone, with an introductory call. If there is a fit, you take it from there.

steps.title: Book your time
steps.body: Choose a slot and reserve your fifteen minutes. Payment is taken at booking.

steps.title: Send your questions
steps.body: Two or three, ahead of the call, so the time goes straight to substance.

steps.title: Get your read
steps.body: A direct conversation, and a clear point of view you can act on.

items.q: How long is the introductory call, and what does it cost?
items.a: A focused fifteen-minute video call for $150, paid at the time of booking.

items.q: What will I actually walk away with?
items.a: A direct, experienced read on the questions you bring, and a clear sense of what you would do next. Come with two or three; you will leave with an answer on each.
Founders scaling past the early days, and funds or investors who want an operator's view on a company, a market, or a thesis. It is built for people who want a sharp point of view, quickly.
Growth strategy, capital strategy, scaling and go-to-market, and building the finance function. This is business and operating guidance, not investment advice.

items.q: What happens to the 5% for charity?
items.a: Five percent of every call goes to organisations working on healthcare for families in South Asia. It is contributed on your behalf, at no extra cost to you.

items.q: Can the introductory call lead to ongoing advisory?
items.a: Yes, and many do. There is no pitch on the call itself. If it is a fit, you will both know, and you take it from there.
Times are shown in your local timezone when you book. Rengan takes calls across US, Canadian, and international hours.
Yes, with reasonable notice ahead of the call. The details are shown at the time of booking.

book.title: Book your 15 minutes.
book.lede: Direct access, your situation, a clear point of view. Choose a time below.
book.giving: Every call gives back. A share of each session supports healthcare for families in South Asia.
